steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: "The author-as-juggler performs a routine" is a complete independent clause. What follows is a three-item list of noun phrases explaining what the routine consists of. The punctuation after "routine" must introduce that list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — routine; the: A semicolon must be followed by an independent clause. "The gathering of central themes..." is a noun phrase, not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice B — routine, the: A comma weakly connects and does not signal the formal introduction of a list elaborating on the preceding clause. ✗
  • Choice C — routine. The: A period ends the sentence, leaving the three noun phrases that follow as a fragment with no verb. ✗
  • Choice D — routine: the: A colon after an independent clause correctly introduces an elaborating list. ✓
That is the correct answer.
